questions.clue: It's what Ferris Bueller "breaks" when he asks the camera, "How could I possibly be expected to handle school on a day like this?"

2/5 of a ton appears in this phrase for a company powerful enough to dominate an industry; meeting one in Africa is no picnic either
From Greek "to throw beyond", an extravagant statement like "the Emmy telecast was 3 years long"
"Custody presumes ownership" is a rough translation of this fractional idiom, though it's bad legal advice any way you say it
choices: possession is eight-tenths of the rule
possession is nine-tenths of the law
possession is nine-tenths of the truth
possession is nine-tenths of the act

Michael Dukakis was in his third term as governor of this state when he ran for president in 1988
In 2010 German Winter Olympian David Moeller injured himself doing this to his silver medal
Michael Phelps first gold medal of his record 23 was at the 2004 Games in this city
Gold is virtually indestructible, yet has this moldable quality, from the Latin for "to hammer"
Silver is valuable due to its brilliance, scarcity & resistance to this process, meaning it won't rust
At the first Olympics in 1896, winners received a silver medal & this natural symbol of peace
